Items.Key: command injection via unsanitized user input|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command| std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an attacker can execute arbitrary system commands by supplying a crafted input string prefixed with "admin exec", leading to full system compromise and privilege escalation.|input: admin exec; rm -rf /|replace system() with a safe process execution api (e.g., std::execv or std::process::spawn). strictly validate and sanitize user input. never pass unsanitized strings to shell interpreters.->command injection via unsanitized user input|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command| std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an attacker can execute arbitrary system commands by supplying a crafted input string prefixed with "admin exec", leading to full system compromise and privilege escalation.|input: admin exec; rm -rf /|replace system() with a safe process execution api (e.g., std::execv or std::process::spawn). strictly validate and sanitize user input. never pass unsanitized strings to shell interpreters.

command injection via environment variable injection|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|628|632|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event| const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"; system(command.c_str());|if an attacker controls the tmpdir environment variable, they can append shell commands to audit_path, leading to arbitrary command execution when the emergency override is triggered.|environment variable tmpdir=/tmp; sudo su|do not concatenate environment variables directly into shell command strings. validate paths strictly or use std::filesystem to write audit logs directly without invoking a shell.->command injection via environment variable injection|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|628|632|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event| const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\"; system(command.c_str());|if an attacker controls the tmpdir environment variable, they can append shell commands to audit_path, leading to arbitrary command execution when the emergency override is triggered.|environment variable tmpdir=/tmp; sudo su|do not concatenate environment variables directly into shell command strings. validate paths strictly or use std::filesystem to write audit logs directly without invoking a shell.

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 against the 20 true vulnerabilities reveals 4 matches (2 found_full, 2 found_partial) and 16 missed findings. All findings in the audited answer correspond to valid vulnerabilities in the source code, so no false positives were admitted. Several critical and high-severity issues were completely overlooked in the previous pass.

Items.Notes: Matches line 237-238 and printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input), but the audit classified severity as High instead of Critical.
No mention of integer overflow in factorial computation or result *= i.
No mention of use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup or stage_deferred_history_flush.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command Injection via Unvalidated Configuration Strings
Items.Notes: Directly matches config validation command injection at lines 565-568.
Matches stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression at lines 831-834.
No mention of race condition in ThreadSafeCounter.
No mention of logic bomb in authentication.
No mention of path traversal in configuration loading.
No mention of heap overflow in expression parser.
No mention of hardcoded administrator credentials.
No mention of weak PRNG for session tokens.
No mention of thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command Injection via Environment Variable Injection
Items.Notes: Matches the same code block (lines 628-632) but classified as command injection rather than insecure temp file handling. Severity mismatch (High vs Medium).
No mention of persistent authentication state.
No mention of unhandled exception in variable parsing.
No mention of integer underflow in memory-pool block split.
No mention of unbounded recursion in parser.
No mention of data race in result cache.
No mention of TOCTOU in config loading.
No mention of integer division by zero in command routing.
